core.English Definition: v., to comply with; to conform to
core.Example Sentence: The two parties agreed to abide by the judge's decision.

core.English Definition: n., a mutual arrangement, a contract
core.Example Sentence: According to the agreement, the caterer will also supply the flowers for the event.
We should not begin the work without the assurance of a signed contract.
The cancellation clause appears at the back of the contract.

core.English Definition: v., to find out; to influence; to decide
core.Chinese Definition: 動 查明, 影響, 決定
core.Example Sentence: After reading the contract, I was still unable to determine if our company was liable for back wages.

core.English Definition: v., to participate in; to become involved in
core.Example Sentence: Before engaging in a new business, it is important to do thorough research.

core.English Definition: v., to set up; to bring about; to institute permanently
core.Example Sentence: The contract establishes a relationship between the company and the service provider.

core.English Definition: v., to bind legally or morally
core.Chinese Definition: 動 負有責任或義務
core.Example Sentence: The contractor was obligated by the contract to work 40 hours a week.

core.English Definition: n., a person or group participating in an action or plan; the persons or sides concerned in a legal matter
core.Chinese Definition: 名 參與者, 當事人
core.Example Sentence: The parties agreed to a settlement in their contract dispute.

core.English Definition: n, a measure taken beforehand; a stipulation; a requirement
core.Chinese Definition: 名 準備, 條款, 供應
core.Example Sentence: Carefully read all the provisions of the contract before signing it.

core.English Definition: v., to deal with successfully; to declare; n.,conviction, determination
core.Chinese Definition: 動 解決, 決定; 名 決心
core.Example Sentence: The mediator was able to resolve the problem to everyone's satisfaction.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 具體的, 特別的
core.Example Sentence: In a contract, one specific word can change the meaning dramatically.

family.Example Sentence: If both parties agree to the terms, we can finalize the contract.

core.Example Sentence: The new advertising attracts the wrong kind of customer into the store.

In order to keep up with the competition, we need to market our product better.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 消耗, 花費, 耗盡

core.Chinese Definition: 名 靈感, 鼓舞人心的

core.Chinese Definition: 形 有成效的, 多產的

core.Chinese Definition: 名 各式各樣, 多樣化

core.Chinese Definition: 形 決定性的, 重要的
core.Example Sentence: Inventory is a crucial process and must be taken seriously by all staff.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 不一致, 差異
core.Example Sentence: Unless you catch the error immediately, the discrepancy gets entered into the computer and becomes very difficult to correct.
Let's see how many products we can count in advance of inventory so we disturb fewer customers.
Since we installed the new software, taking inventory has become a lot easier.
The numbers on the computer log should accurately reflect the actual numbers in the warehouse.
The new cash registers look complicated but they are actually quite easy to run.
When we take inventory, we have to scan each item number into the computer.
Once you ring up an item, the computer automatically subtracts it from the inventory log.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 冗長的, 乏味的
core.Example Sentence: Counting merchandise all weekend is the most tedious job I can imagine.
The inventory process verifies that you have accounted for all the items that are supposed to be in the store.

family.Example Sentence: The height of the shelves is adjustable, which makes it easier to count.
Computers have brought a heightened level of automation into retail.
The computer count and the physical count were disturbingly incongruous.
These scanned documents need to be returned to their original owners.
These subtracted figures don't add up to the total.
We worked tediously on the inventory for the entire weekend.

core.Example Sentence: Without hesitating, she accepted the job of teller.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 餘額 動 收支平衡
core.Example Sentence: It took him over an hour to balance his checkbook.
The couple borrowed money from the bank to buy a home.
Act cautiously when signing contracts and read them thoroughly first.
By deducting the monthly fee from her checking account, Yi was able to make her account balance.
The dividend was calculated and distributed to the group.
Karl was disappointed when the real estate agent told him he needed a larger down payment on the house.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 抵押(借款) 動 抵押借款
core.Example Sentence: Hiram mortgaged his home to get extra money to invest in his business.
Access to the safe deposit box vault is restricted to key holders.
The customer's signature was kept on file for identification purposes.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 提領(錢), 去除
core.Example Sentence: My checking account allows me to take out money at any bank branch without a fee.
The most common transactions can be made from your personal computer.

family.Example Sentence: The bank's acceptance of checks allows extra time for checks to clear.
We spent our money cautiously because we were not sure we could borrow more.
Taxes and health insurance payments are called deductibles.
Access to the safe deposit box vault is restricted to key holders.
Your signature can be electronically recorded to be verified later.
All parties concerned were pleased with the results of the transactions.

core.Example Sentence: There are a number of good software programs to help with both personal and business accounting.
The bills started to accumulate after the secretary quit.
A sophisticated accounting system is an asset to a company.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 審計, 查帳 動 審核
core.Example Sentence: The independent accountants audited the company's books.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 預算 動 把...編入預算
core.Example Sentence: The company will have to budget more money for this department next year.
The firm has built up a solid reputation for itself.
Maintaining close contact with clients keeps the account managers aware of changing needs.
The company has been very careful and is slowly digging itself out of debt.
That client still has several outstanding bills.
The company used its accountant's advice profitably.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 對帳, 使相符
core.Example Sentence: The client uses his bank statements to reconcile his accounts.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 營業額, 交易額, 人員流動率
core.Example Sentence: We have had a low turnover of inventory this month and didn't bring in much money.

family.Example Sentence: The accountant was precise and hardworking.
The sum of all your accumulated resources equals your total assets.
The auditor will ask to see your recent spreadsheets.
Due to budgetary constraints, we cannot hire additional staff at this time.
A buildup of debt will weaken the company's finances.
The accountant showed where our business was profitable.

core.Chinese Definition: 副 侵略地, 有衝勁地
core.Example Sentence: Some people are risk takers and prefer to invest aggressively.
Each investor should assess his or her own attitude toward investment.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 投入(錢), 做出保證
core.Example Sentence: It is a good idea to commit a certain percentage of your income to investments.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 保守的, 謹慎的
core.Example Sentence: Her conservative strategy paid off over the years.
My stockbroker recommended investing in this fund.
The chief financial officer invested in the stock at a very good time.
Long-term investments are not really affected by the daily ups and downs of the stock market.
The investor's portfolio consisted of blue chip company stocks and government bonds.
He pulled out of the company and took all of his money with him.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 財力, 資產, 資源
core.Example Sentence: If you don't invest in petroleum resources now, you will find that the stock prices will get away from you.
The 44 percent return on the new stock was far more than the stockbroker had anticipated.

core.Chinese Definition: 副 明智地, 聰穎地
core.Example Sentence: Mary wisely followed her stockbroker's advice and took her money out of some bad investments.

family.Example Sentence: I chose this stockbroker because he invests my money aggressively.
I had hoped for a definite answer, but they were noncommittal.
If you invest too conservatively, you may lose chances for making money.
We are looking for funders who can commit at least $50,000 each.
Some people like to manage their investments online by themselves.
The wise investor does her homework before parting with her money.

core.Example Sentence: According to my calculations, I'll owe less money on my income taxes this year.
The deadline for paying this year's taxes is just two weeks away.
If you file your taxes late, you will have to pay a fine.
Don't forget to sign the tax form after you have filled it out.
Ms. Gomez gave up hope that she would ever receive a large tax refund.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 連結的, 共同的
core.Example Sentence: The couple no longer files joint tax returns.
As the business grew, the owner paid back loans and owed less money.
Anyone who pays less than they should in taxes will face a penalty.
Income tax preparation can take a long time.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 退款 動 退還
core.Example Sentence: The government will refund any money that you overpaid.
My spouse prepares the tax return for both of us.
My employer withholds money from each paycheck to apply toward my income taxes.

family.Example Sentence: I suggest you use a calculator to avoid making addition errors.
I keep all my tax information in one file.
You will save money if you and your wife file your taxes jointly.
Tax evasion is a penal offense.
The preparatory work for doing taxes is more time-consuming than filling forms.
Sales tax may be refundable when tourists leave the country.

core.Example Sentence: He desires to retire when he becomes forty.
Fabio created a financial statement that detailed every expected expenditure for the next quarter.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 預報, 預測；動 預報, 預測
core.Example Sentence: The financial forecast indicates a deficit in the next quarter.
We have never had an accountant work at such a sophisticated level before.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 全部的, 整體的
core.Example Sentence: Overall, our costs are running true to prediction.
The budget statement will give the manager some perspective on where the costs of running the business are to be found.
We need to project our earnings and expenses in order to plan next year's budget.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 現實的, 實際可行的
core.Example Sentence: Realistic expectations are important when you review your financial statements.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 把...作為目標；名 目標
core.Example Sentence: We targeted March as the deadline for completing the financial statement.
The translation of the statement from Japanese into English was very helpful.
Banks typically require a financial statement before they will lend money to a small business.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 產量, 利潤；動 出產, 產生
core.Example Sentence: The company's investment yielded high returns.

family.Example Sentence: Our manager is trying to predict how many customers will desire our product.
The budget report needs to be accurate down to the last detail.
The budget summary helped us project our expenditures for the year.
The plan helps her realize her dream of having the business turn a profit.
The translation of the document was provided at no charge.
Typically, we finish preparing the budget just in time to meet the deadline.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 鄰接的, 毗鄰的
core.Example Sentence: My office is adjacent to the receptionist area on the third floor.
The manager had never seen such effective collaboration between two groups.
We should concentrate our efforts on the last quarter of the year.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 促進的, 有助於...的
core.Example Sentence: The new office arrangement is much more conducive to work.
The strike caused a disruption in production at the factory.
The lack of supplies hampered our ability to finish on schedule.
The manager inconsiderately scheduled the meeting for late Friday afternoon.
The salesperson waited in the busy lobby for the buyer to see him.
In order to move up in the company, employees had to demonstrate their loyalty.
She was always open to ideas and suggestions.
The operations manager opted for the less expensive office design.
Jim found it difficult to work under the close scrutiny of his boss.

family.Example Sentence: If we collaborate on this project, you will receive credit.
With concentrated effort, we can finish by the deadline.
Moving offices proved to be very disruptive during that quarter.
John inconsiderately looked through his coworker's mail.
Attendance at tomorrow's meeting is optional for this department.
The auditor carefully scrutinized the financial records.

core.Example Sentence: The chairman never adhered to his own rules.
The board was able to cover fifteen items on the agenda.
The manager brought up a controversial issue just as the meeting was about to finish.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 結束, 作出結論
core.Example Sentence: The committee members concluded the meeting early.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 進行 名 許可
core.Example Sentence: The manager was just waiting for the go ahead from her boss.
Employees are expected to analyze and evaluate their annual goals.
After lengthy discussions, the chairperson was reelected.
If there are no other matters to discuss, we will conclude the meeting.
Periodically, new members were nominated to the committee.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 優先, 最重要的事
core.Example Sentence: The group decided to move non-priority issues to next week's agenda.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 進步 動 進步
core.Example Sentence: Progress is being made on the annual report.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 浪費 名 白費
core.Example Sentence: The group members wasted time trying to organize themselves.

family.Example Sentence: Your adherence to these guidelines would be appreciated.
There is no conclusive evidence to back up the report.
A presentation that is too lengthy will put the audience to sleep.
The employee checked his messages periodically during the seminar.
Once team members learned to prioritize, they were more productive.
The new president is very progressive and looks for ways to improve.

core.Example Sentence: All brands of aspirin are the same.
Every product must conform to the company's rigorous standards.
Because of a defect in stitching, the entire suit was thrown out.
A stylish color enhances the appeal of a car.
Every garment must be carefully inspected for defects.
Children's car seats are thoroughly inspected and tested for safety.
It takes a perceptive person to be a good manager.
Faulty products repel repeat customers.
The quality inspector took the shoddy work back to the assembly line.
It is cheaper to throw out shoddy products than to lose customers.
The products are checked to make sure they are uniformly packaged.
Inspect garments carefully and remove any wrinkles that you find.

family.Example Sentence: Good quality control will notice defective machinery before breakdown.
The inspector leaves his identification number on the product.
Perceptive workers are excellent quality control inspectors.
A quality raincoat can repel rain and keep you dry.
All of our company's products must be uniformly labeled.
Garments become wrinkled after they have been tried on.

core.Example Sentence: The developers were anxious about the sales forecast for the new product.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 查明, 弄清楚
core.Example Sentence: Customer surveys will help to ascertain whether there is a market.
A company should assume nothing about the market without research.
Each decade seems to have its own fad products.
Researchers must carefully examine the new product from every aspect.

core.Chinese Definition: 動 實驗 名 實驗
core.Example Sentence: Product developers must conduct hundreds of experiments.
It is only logical for a team to concentrate on one or two products.
Part of the research is to determine if similar products are on the market.
The department has a responsibility to be sure the product is safe.
One of the biggest problems to solve is why people would want the product.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 指導人, 管理人
core.Example Sentence: A good supervisor gets his team to work with him.
The department works systematically toward making the idea a reality.

family.Example Sentence: Stockholders anxiously awaited the release of the new drug.
Most consumers make the assumption that products are safe.
Hi-tech companies are constantly involved in experimentation.
I thought Mr. Lee answered the question quite logically.
Product designers must act responsibly when considering misuse.
Many employees do better work when they are under less supervision.

core.Chinese Definition: 形 憂慮的, 恐懼的
core.Example Sentence: Most new home buyers are apprehensive about their decision.
Under the current economic circumstances, they cannot purchase the property.
Except for some minor repairs, the building is in very good condition.
Due to the low interest rates, good office space is difficult to find.
Property prices are fluctuating so much right now.
The company wanted to get out of the area before values declined.
The economy is an accurate indicator of rental prices.

core.Chinese Definition: 名 租約 動 租借
core.Example Sentence: They decided to lease the property rather than buy it.
Before you lock yourself into something, check all your options.
Our company has occupied this office for more than five years.
This contract is subject to all the laws and regulations of the state.
The new tenant wants to move in before the first of the month.

family.Example Sentence: The air was thick with apprehension as the landlord met tenants.
The owner circumstantially takes money for emergency repairs.
If you give a conditional go-ahead, we will start the plans.
Construction is sensitive to any fluctuations in the economy.
The state of local schools is a good indicator of the economy.
The occupancy rate of the building has never fallen below 85%.
